county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Adams County formed 1848 from Portage County. County seat is Friendship. Central Wisconsin sand prairie county. Predominantly agricultural with tourism around Castle Rock Lake and Petenwell Lake.
county_metadata.research_tips: Adams County Register of Deeds in Friendship holds land records from 1848. Wisconsin Historical Society (WHS) in Madison has microfilmed Adams County records including deeds, probate, and vital records. Adams County was part of the central Wisconsin sand prairie -- settlers included Bohemian (Czech) and German families. Check WHS for early territorial-era land entries via BLM GLO patents. Church records (Catholic, Lutheran) at local parishes and WHS supplement civil records. The Adams County Historical Society in Friendship maintains local family files and photographs. FamilySearch has digitized some Adams County records. Castle Rock and other natural features attracted early settlers whose families can be tracked through successive census enumerations.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Adams County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Friendship; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Adams County from 1848; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1848; no major record losses

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Adams County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ashland County formed 1860 from La Pointe (unorganized territory). County seat is Ashland on Chequamegon Bay of Lake Superior. Lumber, mining, and commercial fishing heritage. Bad River Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ashland County Register of Deeds in Ashland holds land records from 1860. Originally part of the La Pointe unorganized territory. Ashland sits on Chequamegon Bay of Lake Superior -- lumber and mining industries brought diverse immigrant workers (Finnish, Swedish, Italian, Polish, Cornish). The Bad River Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation is in Ashland County -- tribal enrollment and allotment records at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). WHS has microfilmed Ashland County records. The Ashland Historical Society Museum holds local collections. Northern Great Lakes Visitor Center has regional history resources. Lumber company records at WHS and the Forest History Society document workers and families. FamilySearch microfilm available.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ashland County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ashland; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1860; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Barron County formed 1859 as Dallas County (renamed Barron 1869). County seat is Barron. Northwestern Wisconsin agricultural and lumber county. Named for Henry D. Barron, Wisconsin politician.
county_metadata.research_tips: Barron County Register of Deeds in Barron holds land records from 1859. Originally named Dallas County (renamed Barron 1869). Northwestern Wisconsin lumber and farming county. Strong Norwegian and German immigrant communities -- check Norwegian-American Historical Association (NAHA) at St. Olaf College for emigration records. WHS has microfilmed Barron County records. The Barron County Historical Society in Cameron maintains local collections. Pioneer Village Museum in Cameron documents early settlement. Church records (Norwegian Lutheran, German Lutheran, Catholic) are important supplements. FamilySearch has indexed some Barron County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Barron County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Barron; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bayfield County formed 1845 from La Pointe (unorganized territory). County seat is Washburn. Northernmost county in Wisconsin on Lake Superior. Apostle Islands National Lakeshore. Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Bayfield County Register of Deeds in Washburn holds land records from settlement era. Located on the northern tip of Wisconsin along Lake Superior, including the Apostle Islands. The Red Cliff Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation is in Bayfield County -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Commercial fishing, logging, and brownstone quarrying brought diverse settlers (Scandinavian, Finnish, French-Canadian). WHS has microfilmed Bayfield County records. The Bayfield Heritage Association and Washburn Area Historical Society hold local collections. Madeline Island Museum has fur trade-era and early settlement records. FamilySearch microfilm available. BLM GLO patents document original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Bayfield County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Washburn; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brown County formed 1818 as one of Wisconsin's two original counties (with Crawford, under Michigan Territory). County seat is Green Bay, one of the oldest European settlements in the Midwest. French fur trade from the 1600s. Oneida Nation reservation partly in county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Brown County Register of Deeds in Green Bay holds land records from 1818 -- one of Wisconsin's two original counties (with Crawford). Green Bay is one of the oldest European settlements in the Midwest, with fur trade records from the French regime (1600s). Brown County Historical Society in Green Bay has extensive manuscript collections. Neville Public Museum has local history resources. The Oneida Nation reservation is partly in Brown County -- tribal records at NARA. Very strong Belgian (Walloon) immigrant community in Door-Kewaunee-Brown corner -- Heritage Hill State Historical Park preserves Belgian settlement. German and Dutch immigrants also prominent. WHS has microfilmed Brown County records extensively. Catholic Diocese of Green Bay archives hold parish registers from the 1820s. FamilySearch has many Brown County collections indexed.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Brown County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Green Bay; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1818; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Buffalo County formed 1853 from unorganized territory (Trempealeau). County seat is Alma, perched on Mississippi River bluffs. Swiss, German, and Norwegian settlement. Agricultural heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Buffalo County Register of Deeds in Alma holds land records from 1853. Located along the Mississippi River bluffs in western Wisconsin. Swiss and German immigration was significant -- the village of Alma was named for a Crimean War battle by German settlers. Norwegian and Polish settlers also established communities. WHS has microfilmed Buffalo County records. The Buffalo County Historical Society in Alma maintains local collections. Church records (German Lutheran, German Catholic, Norwegian Lutheran) supplement civil records. FamilySearch microfilm available. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Steamboat-era records at WHS document river commerce and immigration.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Buffalo County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Alma; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Burnett County formed 1856 from unorganized territory (Douglas). County seat is Siren. Northwestern Wisconsin forest and lake county. St. Croix Chippewa Indians of Wisconsin reservation partly in county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Burnett County Register of Deeds in Siren holds land records from 1856. Northwestern Wisconsin county with extensive lakes and forests. The St. Croix Chippewa Indians of Wisconsin reservation is partly in Burnett County -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region. Lumber industry records document early workers and families. Swedish, Norwegian, and Finnish settlers established farming communities. WHS has microfilmed Burnett County records. Burnett County Historical Society in Siren maintains local files. Governor Knowles State Forest area. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. FamilySearch has some indexed Burnett County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Burnett County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Siren; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Calumet County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Chilton. Eastern Wisconsin county between Lake Winnebago and Lake Michigan. Very strong German (Catholic) settlement. St. Nazianz religious commune (1854).
county_metadata.research_tips: Calumet County Register of Deeds in Chilton holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Strong German (Catholic) settlement -- Calumet County had one of the highest proportions of German-born residents in the state. Stockbridge-Munsee community (Mohican) was located here before removal to Shawano County. WHS has microfilmed Calumet County records. The Calumet County Historical Society in New Holstein maintains collections. Catholic parish records in the Green Bay diocese (Chilton, New Holstein, St. Nazianz) are critical for German Catholic family research. St. Nazianz was founded as a religious commune in 1854 by Father Ambrose Oschwald. FamilySearch has indexed many Calumet County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Calumet County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Chilton; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Chippewa County formed 1845 from Crawford County. County seat is Chippewa Falls on the Chippewa River. One of Wisconsin's premier lumber industry counties. Strong German and Scandinavian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Chippewa County Register of Deeds in Chippewa Falls holds land records from 1845. Named for the Chippewa River -- one of Wisconsin's premier lumber industry counties. The Leinenkugel brewery (1867) and other German enterprises document working families. Very strong German and Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish) immigrant communities. WHS has microfilmed Chippewa County records extensively. The Chippewa County Historical Society in Chippewa Falls has local collections. Lumber baron mansions and company records at WHS and in local archives. Catholic and Lutheran church records supplement civil records. FamilySearch has indexed some Chippewa County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Chippewa County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Chippewa Falls; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Chippewa County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Chippewa County Register of Deeds, Chippewa Falls
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Chippewa County from 1845; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1845; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Chippewa County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Chippewa County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Chippewa County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Chippewa County Probate Court, Chippewa Falls
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Chippewa County from 1845; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Chippewa County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Chippewa County Clerk, Chippewa Falls / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Chippewa County from 1845; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Chippewa County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Chippewa County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Chippewa County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Chippewa County Lumber Industry Records -- WHS
source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Lumber company records for Chippewa County at WHS including employment ledgers, payrolls, company store accounts, logging camp records, and land holdings; essential for tracing workers in the northern Wisconsin timber industry; circuit court records also document labor disputes and land claims

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clark County formed 1853 from Marathon County. County seat is Neillsville. Central Wisconsin lumber and farming county. Strong German settlement. Old Order Amish communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clark County Register of Deeds in Neillsville holds land records from 1853. Central Wisconsin county with strong German (Catholic and Lutheran) settlement. The Clark County Historical Society in Neillsville maintains local collections. WHS has microfilmed Clark County records. Lumber industry was important in northern Clark County. Old Order Amish communities settled in Clark County beginning in the late 1800s -- check Budget newspaper (Sugarcreek, OH) for Amish community news. Church records (Catholic, Lutheran, Amish) are essential supplements. FamilySearch has some Clark County records indexed. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Highground Veterans Memorial Park near Neillsville.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Clark County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Neillsville; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
